compiled_state.title: As a fix-bug orchestrator, I can detect feature-request language in bug tickets before investigation
compiled_state.author: Joe Oakhart
compiled_state.description:
## Description

**What**: Add a feature-vs-bug language check (Gate 1b) that fires after the intent search returns "ambiguous". Analyzes the bug ticket title and description for language patterns indicating a feature request rather than a bug report.
**Why**: Bugs filed with language like "doesn't support X" or "missing X capability" are often feature requests, not broken behavior. Flagging these before investigation prevents wasted effort on implementing new functionality under the guise of a bug fix.
**Scope**:
- IN: Language pattern matching on ticket title and description; "doesn't support/accept/handle X" and "missing X capability" patterns as primary signals; test suite of 10+ ticket descriptions with known correct classifications
- OUT: Intent search (Story 1); configurable patterns (future enhancement); escalation routing (Story 6)

## Done Definitions

- When this story is complete, bugs whose ticket title or description matches feature-request language patterns are flagged as potential feature requests before investigation begins
  ← Satisfies: "Bugs whose ticket title or description matches feature-request language patterns are flagged as potential feature requests before investigation begins"
- When this story is complete, a test suite of 10+ ticket descriptions with known correct classifications verifies the pattern matching
  ← Satisfies: "verified by a test suite of 10+ ticket descriptions with known correct classifications"
- When this story is complete, unit tests written and passing for all new or modified logic
  ← TDD Done-of-Done

## Considerations

- [Testing] Test corpus should include edge cases: domain-specific "handle/support" language that describes broken behavior, not missing capability

comments.body: All 4 child tasks completed: gate-1b script, RED tests, and Gate 1b SKILL.md integration.
comments.author: Joe Oakhart
